era_context: Late Bronze Age, mid-18th Dynasty Egypt under Amenhotep II. The New Kingdom is at its territorial peak. In Anatolia, the Hittite Empire is consolidating under Tudhaliya III. In the Aegean, Mycenaean civilization is flourishing in Late Helladic IIIA. In Mesopotamia, Kassite Babylon is the dominant power. In China, the Shang Dynasty is in its middle period.
geographic_coverage_gaps: East Asia (Chinese sources are problematic for this period)
South Asia (Indus Valley Civilization had collapsed; Vedic period is poorly dated)

events.title: Amenhotep II's Reign Consolidation
events.description: Amenhotep II was consolidating his authority as pharaoh during the mid-18th Dynasty. His reign is characterized by military campaigns, temple construction, and the maintenance of Egyptian hegemony over Nubia and the Levant. He was known for his athletic prowess and military reputation, which were emphasized in temple inscriptions and reliefs.

sources.name: Temple inscriptions at Karnak, Luxor, and Amada

events.certainty_note: Amenhotep II's reign is well-attested in Egyptian sources, but the precise dating of individual years within his reign is uncertain. Conventional chronology places his reign c. 1427–1400 BCE.

disconfirming_evidence: Some scholars dispute the conventional dating of Amenhotep II's reign. The 'Low Chronology' would place him later (c. 1390–1365 BCE), while the 'High Chronology' would place him earlier (c. 1450–1425 BCE). This introduces ±50 year uncertainty into the dating of events attributed to his reign. Additionally, some scholars question the extent of Egyptian military success claimed in temple inscriptions.
historiographic_note: The historical record for this year is dominated by Egyptian royal inscriptions, which are propagandistic and elite-authored. The dating of events relies on regnal chronology that is itself debated. No contemporary sources exist from other regions that would provide alternative perspectives on events in Egypt or the broader Mediterranean world.

graph_edges.note: Consolidation of Amenhotep II's authority was part of his broader military and political program
